title:
Design System
Migration
subtitle: Moving Align's design system from Figma to Claude Code.

slides.title:
Design System
Migration
slides.description: We moved Align's design system from a Figma library to Claude Code — turning components into code the team can prompt, generate, and ship directly.

slides.label: The Problem
slides.title: Figma said one thing. Production said another.
slides.content: The system lived in Figma, but every handoff introduced drift. By the time components reached users, they didn't match what was designed.
issues: Figma components and shipped code drifted every release
Handoffs required re-specifying what was already visible in Figma
Token updates in Figma never reached the product
Designers maintained a system that wasn't the source of truth
slides.highlight: The design system was a reference — not the thing that shipped.

slides.label: Why Claude Code
slides.title: If the code is the product, the system should live in code.
slides.content: Figma is built for exploration — not for being the source of truth. Claude Code collapses the design-to-code gap: designers describe a component, Claude generates it in the real stack, and the output is the system itself.
slides.highlight: Figma shows what it should look like. Claude Code ships what it actually is.

slides.label: Design Goals
slides.title: What the migration had to deliver.

goals.title: One source of truth
goals.description: Components in code — Figma becomes exploration, not reference.

goals.title: Close the handoff gap
goals.description: Design decisions ship without a spec in between.

goals.title: Docs that write themselves
goals.description: Every component ships with its own usage guidelines.

kpis: Time from design to shipped component

slides.label: The Pipeline
slides.title: From Figma handoff to Claude-generated components

beforeImage.caption: Old — Design → Spec → Build → QA → Drift

slides.beforeDescription: Designers specced in Figma, engineers rebuilt in code, QA chased the gaps. Every step introduced translation loss.
slides.afterDescription: Designers prompt Claude with intent and tokens. Claude generates the component in the real stack — ready to review and ship.
beforeBullets: Manual spec writing for every component
afterBullets: Claude generates directly in the codebase
Prompts capture intent — versioned and reviewable
Review happens on the real component
slides.highlight: The handoff didn't get faster. It stopped existing.

slides.label: Tokens & Components
slides.title: One token source. 40+ components rebuilt.
slides.content: Colour, spacing, typography, and motion tokens live in one place and flow through Claude Code into every component. We rebuilt the core library — buttons, inputs, modals, cards, tables — each with variants, states, a11y, and docs generated alongside.
issues: Three-layer token model — primitive → semantic → component
40+ core components generated and reviewed through Claude Code
Usage docs and Storybook entries generated from the same prompts
Token changes reviewed like code — with diffs and history
slides.highlight: Components ship with their docs. Tokens update everywhere at once.

image.caption: Token architecture and core component library

slides.label: Proof in Product
slides.title: Rebuilding iTero screens on the new system
slides.content: We rebuilt three of the most complex iTero screens — patient report, scan workflow, and clinician dashboard — using only the new library and Claude Code. Every screen came together faster, with less custom code, and matched the design pixel-for-pixel.
slides.highlight: What used to take a sprint shipped in an afternoon — with no drift.

image.caption: iTero screens rebuilt with the new system

slides.label: Team Feedback
slides.quote: I stopped writing specs. I describe what I want, Claude builds it in our stack, and we review the real thing.
slides.author: Senior Product Designer

slides.label: Outcomes
slides.title: A system that ships what it designs.

outcomes.title: Design-to-shipped
outcomes.description: Components that took weeks now generate and ship in hours.

outcomes.title: Figma-to-code drift
outcomes.description: The code is the system — nothing to drift from.

outcomes.title: Component reuse
outcomes.description: Teams pull from the same library — no one-off implementations.

outcomes.title: Designer-engineer alignment
outcomes.description: Both sides work in the same medium — nothing lost in translation.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.subtitle: Building design systems that actually ship.
